fields.description:
# S2 — Deploy Gerrit + rebar MCP (docker-compose) behind nginx + TLS

## Why
Run Gerrit and the rebar MCP as the two containers on the box, reachable over HTTPS at
`rebar.solutions.navateam.com`. Gerrit needs a `%2F`-safe reverse proxy — the community
uses nginx/Apache, NOT Caddy (Caddy URL-decodes paths by default with no global switch,
which breaks Gerrit's change/project screens; no confirmed Caddy+Gerrit recipe exists).
This story is the bottom layer of the d251 "proven pipe": Gerrit serving, the MCP voter
reachable over HTTP, TLS terminated, secrets sourced from SSM. The downstream stories
(S3 labels/submit-reqs, S4 bot + event wiring, S5 replication) assume an HTTP-reachable
MCP endpoint, so S2 must close the transport gap rather than assert it.

## What
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`:
  - official `gerritcodereview/gerrit` pinned to a PATCH tag (e.g. `3.14.1`) with
    persistent volumes (git/index/cache/etc/db on the data volume) + the two-step init.
    DERISK NOTE (E4): the bare minor tags `3.14`/`3.13` do NOT exist on Docker Hub; pin a
    published patch tag (`3.14.1`/`3.13.7`/`3.12.8`), all of which publish arm64 manifests.
  - the `rebar-mcp` container (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM=1`, signing key + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
    sourced from SSM into the boot `.env` — see secrets bullet below; write-gating as needed);
    both `restart: unless-stopped`, pinned tags.
  - the Gerrit data volume is declared **`external: true`** (created once, outside compose)
    so a stray `docker compose down -v` cannot destroy git/NoteDb/index state. Freeze-and-
    restore (the d251 recovery posture, no break-glass v1) is exercised by snapshotting this
    volume; a nightly host-level snapshot/`tar` of the data volume is wired by a bootstrap
    timer and the restore procedure is documented in the runbook.
- **rebar-mcp HTTP transport (in-scope S2 deliverable).** rebar-mcp is currently
  **stdio-only** (`build_server().run()`, no transport arg; FastMCP defaults to stdio) —
  nginx cannot proxy a stdio process. S2 resolves this by giving rebar-mcp an HTTP surface:
  the MCP container entrypoint launches it over an HTTP/SSE (FastMCP `streamable-http`)
  transport bound to `0.0.0.0:<MCP_PORT>` (default `8000`) **OR**, if the pinned FastMCP
  version does not export `streamable-http`, a thin in-repo HTTP webhook/vote receiver
  (`src/rebar/mcp_http.py`, an ASGI shim exposing the Set-Review/vote path the Gerrit voter
  calls) is added and run instead. The transport choice is recorded in
  `docs/adr/0002-rebar-mcp-http-transport.md` (FastMCP-version check is the deciding input).
  nginx routes `location /mcp/ { proxy_pass http://rebar-mcp:<MCP_PORT>/; }`.
- `gerrit.config`: `container.heapLimit=2g` with `core.packedGitLimit` budgeted INSIDE the
  heap (~512m-1g), NoteDb, `canonicalWebUrl=https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/`, sshd/httpd
  thread caps + `sshd.batchThreads` to avoid parallel-clone OOM.
- nginx + certbot (Let's Encrypt) terminating TLS on 443: route Gerrit (`%2F`-safe:
  `proxy_pass` with no path) and the MCP webhook/vote endpoint (`location /mcp/`) behind the
  one cert; Gerrit SSH passthrough on 29418.
- **certbot renewal lifecycle.** Initial issuance AND renewal are wired: a systemd timer
  (or cron) runs `certbot renew` twice daily with a deploy/post-hook that reloads nginx
  (`certbot renew --quiet --deploy-hook 'nginx -s reload'`, or the containerized equivalent
  `docker exec nginx nginx -s reload`); nginx serves an `/.well-known/acme-challenge/`
  location (webroot) so HTTP-01 renewal succeeds. Installed/enabled by the bootstrap script.
- **secrets bootstrap (SSM → ephemeral `.env`).** A reviewed bootstrap script,
  `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`, runs `aws ssm get-parameter --with-decryption` for the
  signing key + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (authenticating via the **instance role**, no static keys)
  and writes them to a root-only (`chmod 0600`) `.env` that docker-compose reads, BEFORE
  `docker compose up`. The `.env` is **gitignored and ephemeral** (regenerated on every
  boot/redeploy, never committed). The script is idempotent on re-run and **aborts** if SSM
  is unreachable (never silently reuses a stale `.env`); the bootstrap timer re-fetches on
  reboot so rotated keys are picked up. This `.env`-from-SSM bridge is the canonical secrets
  source and is recorded in `docs/adr/0003-secrets-source-ssm.md`.
- **IAM instance role (least privilege).** The host's instance profile grants
  `ssm:GetParameter`/`GetParameters` scoped to the project parameter-path prefix only
  (e.g. `arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:896586841071:parameter/rebar/prod/*`) plus `kms:Decrypt` on
  the SecureString key — no wildcard `parameter/*` grant. Defined in the infra IaC.
- **observability (minimal).** Both containers log via a host log driver (`journald`); a
  single uptime/health probe hits `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/` and the MCP
  `/mcp/` health path; a disk-usage alarm guards the Gerrit data volume.

## Scope
In: containers, volumes (external data volume + nightly snapshot), base `gerrit.config`,
nginx+TLS+certbot renewal, MCP HTTP transport surface + hosting, SSM→.env secrets bootstrap,
the SSM IAM instance role, minimal logging/health/disk observability. Out: Gerrit
project/label/submit-req (S3), bot identity + event wiring (S4), replication (S5).

## Files
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`, `infra/compose/gerrit.config`, `infra/nginx/*.conf`
  (incl. the `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` location and the `location /mcp/` upstream)
- `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` (SSM → ephemeral `.env`), bootstrap scripts under `infra/`
  (install the certbot-renew timer, the nightly volume-snapshot timer, the health/disk checks)
- `src/rebar/mcp_http.py` (the HTTP/webhook transport shim) **or** the entrypoint flag that
  switches rebar-mcp to FastMCP `streamable-http` — whichever the ADR selects
- `docs/adr/0002-rebar-mcp-http-transport.md`, `docs/adr/0003-secrets-source-ssm.md`
- `.gitignore` (ensure `infra/compose/.env` is ignored)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gerrit is reachable at `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/` with a valid Let's Encrypt cert.
- [ ] A Gerrit change screen / a project path containing `%2F` renders correctly (encoded-slash round-trip verified end-to-end through nginx).
- [ ] certbot renewal is automated: the systemd timer/cron for `certbot renew` is installed and enabled, the nginx `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` location serves the HTTP-01 challenge, and a `certbot renew --dry-run` succeeds and reloads nginx via the deploy/post-hook.
- [ ] Gerrit SSH clone works on 29418 and the admin account is bootstrapped.
- [ ] Gerrit data persists across `docker compose down/up` and an instance reboot (`restart: unless-stopped`); the data volume is declared `external: true` so `docker compose down -v` does not destroy it.
- [ ] The rebar-mcp container exposes an HTTP/SSE (or webhook) endpoint bound to `0.0.0.0:<MCP_PORT>` and is reachable through nginx at `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/mcp/` (a request reaches the MCP process, not a 502); the transport choice (FastMCP `streamable-http` vs HTTP shim) is recorded in `docs/adr/0002-rebar-mcp-http-transport.md`.
- [ ] The rebar-mcp container's signing key + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are sourced from SSM at boot by `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` into a `0600`, gitignored, ephemeral `.env` (never baked into the image/compose, never committed); the script authenticates via the instance role, is idempotent, and aborts if SSM is unreachable.
- [ ] The SSM IAM instance role grants `GetParameter`/`GetParameters` scoped to `parameter/rebar/prod/*` only (no wildcard), verified against the attached policy.
- [ ] Both containers log to the host log driver (`journald`); an uptime/health probe covers `/` and `/mcp/`, and a disk-usage alarm covers the Gerrit data volume.
- [ ] Gerrit runs a supported version at an EXISTING arm64 patch tag (e.g. `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1`; a bare minor tag like `3.14` is NOT used — it doesn't exist), with `heapLimit=2g` and `packedGitLimit` budgeted within it.
